summary: Shared practical knowledge and rumors commonly available to blacksmiths in the Old Market.
known_facts: The Merchant Guild pays promptly for standardized fittings and caravan repairs.
Guards inspect cellar doors more often near the Reincarnation Festival.
Archive seals can delay the sale of old metalwork until provenance is checked.
rumors: Some ruin-recovered blades will not hold a new edge unless quenched under moonlight.
A sealed well below the market echoes with hammering on festival nights.
unknowns: The true purpose of the underground inscriptions.
Which guild factors secretly trade in restricted artifacts.
notes: Blacksmith knowledge skews toward material evidence and workshop gossip.
